folio.label: 76r
folio.l: Who wold wene , or who wold suppose
The woo that in myn hert was , & the pyne
And whan I saw , he wold neuäe fyne
To reden on this cursed book , al nygút
Al sodeynly , three leeues haue I pligút
Out of his booke , rigút´ as he radde , & eke
I witú my fist , so toke hi~ on the cheke
That in oure fyr , he fel bakward a dounö
And he vp stirt , as dotú a wood lyouö
And witú his fist , he smot me on the hede
That in the floor , I lay as I were dede
And whan he saw , how stille that I lay
He was a gast , and wold han fled a way
Til at the last , out of my swougú I brayde
O hast æè slayn me , fals theef I sayde
And for my lond , thus hast æè mordred me
Or I be ded , ©et wole I kysse the
And nere he com , and kneled faire a doun
And seide deere Suster , Alisounö
As helpe me god , I shal the neuäe smyte
That I haue doon , it is thy self to wyte
For©eue it me , and that I the byseke
And ©et eftsones , I hitte hi~ on the cheke
And seide thef , thus meche am I a wreke
Now wol I dye , I may no lengere speke
But at the last , witú mechil kare & woo
We fel accorded , be oure seluen twoo
He ©af me al the brydel , in myn honde
To han the gouänaunce , of hous & londe
And of his tonge , and of his hond also
And mad hi~ brenne his book , a noon rigút thoo
And wha~ne that I had getyn , vn to me
Be maistrye , al the soueraynte
And that he seide , myn owen trewe wyf
Do as the list , the terme of al thy lyf
Kepe thyn honour , & kepe eke myn estate
And after that day , we hadden neuäe debate
God help me so , I was to hi~ as kynde
As any wyf , fro Denmark vn to Inde
And also trewe , and so was he to me
I preye to god , that sittetú in mageste
So blisse his soule , for his mercy deere
Now wol I seyn my tale , if ©e wyln heere
¢ The Frere lougú , whan he had herd al this

note.notetxt: 803There is a cross in dry point in the right margin, just under the marginalia with the blue paraph.ODR

folio.l: Now dame q¶ he , so haue I ioye or blys
This is a long preamble , of a tale
And whan the Sompnour , herd the Frere gale
Loo q¶ the Sompnour , for goddes armes two

note.notetxt: 807The last three lines are crammed, even though in the following page there should have been planty of space. If he was copying in sequence, he could have used some of the space in the verso to accomodate his text. Check out for copying technique. See former quires.ODR
